mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when the economy operates above trend.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth yields negative or flat 20-day equity returns during mid-cycle expansion phases, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 195 signal entrenched inflationary pressures that force aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 195 consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting higher wage growth and consumer spending that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of observations during walk-forward validation, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, directly translating to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward equity returns across multiple business cycles, with a rolling Rank IC persistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 1.0 percent indicates severe supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops above the 1.0 percent threshold consistently precede positive 5-day equity returns, or the threshold signal yields a negative predictive accuracy below 50%.
